attack_explanation: The attacker authenticated as 'admin', read /etc/shadow, then downloaded a Mirai-style payload to /tmp and triggered a planted AWS key.
session_summary: 132s SSH session from a Tor exit node; credential access + ingress tool transfer; engaged the FinServ decoy and burned a canary.
mitre_explanation: T1110 (brute force) → T1003.008 (shadow read) → T1105 (tool transfer). C2 not confirmed.
risk_explanation: High risk: hands-on operator, successful auth, malware staging, and canary use within a single session.
explain_why: Score 78.5 driven by successful credential use, malware download, and canary trigger; tier classified advanced_human.

recommended_responses.rationale: Contain the live session and preserve forensics.
Block the source IP at the firewall after capture.

generated_insights: Behaviour matches a known SSH-bruteforce botnet cluster; likely part of a broader campaign.
executive_summary: A skilled intruder breached a decoy host, attempted credential theft and malware staging, and was safely contained by the deception layer.

analyst_notes.note: Confirmed canary trigger; escalating to campaign review.
